**What it shows.** Reduced-size IIIF thumbnail of folio 23v from BL
Cotton Vespasian A.i (the Old English Bede), showing both columns of
text and the illuminated initial that opens the right column.

**How to read it.** The transcription apparatus is anchored to the
folio's two-column layout — left column lines L1–L26, right column
lines R1–R26 — and the variant at line L7 (logged in the editorial
notes) is visible just below the midpoint of the left column.

**Why it matters.** The thumbnail provides the visual reference for
the apparatus criticus and lets reviewers spot-check the
transcription against the source without paging through the full
IIIF tile pyramid. The fuller-resolution tiles live at the IIIF
endpoint declared in the manifest frontmatter.
